const renderOverview = () => (

nextSTEP High School

Fort Saskatchewan Sherwood Park Vegreville

👋 Welcome! Your progress is automatically saved to your browser as you work. When you are finished, click the Final Review tab to verify your answers.

Career & Life Management

CALM is a compulsory course for Alberta High School students. It is the final component of the grade 1-12 Health Promotion Program. It is a 3 credit course, and is broken up into 3 modules.

The aim of CALM is to enable students to make well informed, considered decisions and choices in all aspects of their lives.

The 3 Pillars of Life Choices:

1
Personal Choices Apply understanding of well-being and personal health to daily teenage decisions.
2
Resource Choices Understand and make responsible decisions in the use of your resources.
3
Career & Life Choices Develop processes for managing personal and lifelong career growth.
);

A goal without a plan is just a wish! To make sure you actually hit your targets, use the SMART method. Your goals should be:

  • Specific: What exactly are you trying to do? (Don't just say "get better at guitar", say "learn to play Wonderwall on guitar").
  • Measurable: How will you know when you've succeeded?
  • Attainable: Is it within your power to do this, or does it rely on luck/other people?
  • Realistic: Do you have the resources, skill, and time to pull this off right now?
  • Timed: When is the exact deadline?
updateForm('goalAchieve', e.target.value)} />

                Evaluating Relationships
              </SectionTitle>

{[0, 1, 2, 3, 4].map(idx => (
{idx + 1}
updateArrayField('evalRels', idx, e.target.value)} />

The Vibe Check

✅ Green Flags (Healthy)
  • They respect your boundaries and the word "No".
  • You feel energized and happy after hanging out with them.
  • They celebrate your wins and encourage your goals.
  • You can communicate openly without fear of them exploding.
🚩 Red Flags (Unhealthy)
  • They try to isolate you from your other friends or family.
  • They constantly make you feel guilty or "crazy" (gaslighting).
  • They pressure you into doing things you aren't comfortable with.
  • The relationship feels like a rollercoaster (extreme highs and terrible lows).

Green Flags

{[0, 1, 2, 3, 4].map(idx => ( updateArrayField('evalHealthyList', idx, e.target.value)} /> ))}

Red Flags

{[0, 1, 2, 3, 4].map(idx => ( updateArrayField('evalUnhealthyList', idx, e.target.value)} /> ))}

const renderAlcohol = () => (
            <Card>
              <SectionTitle subtitle="Navigating the choices and consequences surrounding drinking.">
                Alcohol Awareness
              </SectionTitle>
Action & Consequence

{['Not to Drink', 'Drink Moderately', 'Drink Too Much'].map((choice, idx) => (
The Choice The Good (Pros) The Bad (Cons)
{choice}

Tobacco and most vape juices contain nicotine, a highly addictive chemical that rewires how your developing brain handles stress and reward. People develop a tolerance quickly, needing more just to feel "normal".

Vaping carries serious risks for lung health and delivers massive nicotine concentrations. Quitting is tough but highly possible with support!

AlbertaQuits: 1-866-710-QUIT
KidsHelpPhone: Text CONNECT to 686868

{riskQuestions.map((q, idx) => (

{q}

setFormData(prev => ({ ...prev, riskRatings: { ...prev.riskRatings, [idx]: val } }))} />
))}

When we hear the word "risk", we usually think of danger. But growing up requires taking risks! The trick is knowing the difference:

  • 📈 Healthy Risks: Pushing yourself outside your comfort zone to grow. The "worst-case scenario" might be feeling embarrassed or failing, but you won't get hurt. (e.g., trying out for a play, applying for a hard job, standing up for a friend).
  • ☠️ Unhealthy Risks: Actions that are needlessly dangerous, illegal, or could cause long-term physical/mental harm to you or others. (e.g., driving drunk, trying addictive drugs, sharing inappropriate photos online).

Healthy Risks

Growth-oriented activities

{[0, 1, 2, 3, 4].map(idx => ( updateArrayField('riskHealthy', idx, e.target.value)} /> ))}

Unhealthy Risks

Needlessly dangerous

{[0, 1, 2, 3, 4].map(idx => ( updateArrayField('riskUnhealthy', idx, e.target.value)} /> ))}
The Addiction Continuum

1
No Use

No use due to age, religion, or health risks.

2
Use

Experimentation or "social use" to enhance good experiences.

3
Misuse

Experiencing negative consequences (trouble, regret) but ignoring them.

4
Dependency

Interferes with major life areas. No longer a choice.

Scenario Analysis

Scenario 1: Rachel can't wait to finish school so she can get home and start drinking. Every day after school, Rachel has at least 5 beers to mellow her out. Her friends and family have expressed concern. She has a hard time sleeping without it.
Scenario 2: Antoine has never touched a cigarette. After losing his father to lung cancer, Antoine has decided that he will never try one.
Scenario 3: Bailey has a craving. She wants one, but doesn't have one. She notices a half full pack on the table beside her, and decides to steal one when no one is looking.

For more information, or to find support for addictions, contact Alberta Health Services (AHS):

Addiction Help Line: 1-866-332-2322

); const renderMentalHealth = () => ( Mental Health

You are not alone

When you're depressed, it can feel like no one understands. But depression is far more common in teens than you may think. It is not a hopeless case. With proper treatment and healthy choices, that fog eventually lifts.

Warning Signs

  • 🚨 Irritable, sad, or angry
  • 🚨 Nothing seems fun anymore
  • 🚨 Feeling worthless or guilty
  • 🚨 Sleeping too much or not enough
  • 🔥 Thinking about death or suicide. (Seek help immediately!)
How to fight back

1. One day at a time Start with small goals. Stay connected.
2. Don't isolate Spend time with upbeat friends. Avoid toxic people.
3. Body Health Exercise releases endorphins. Eat good food.
4. Ask for help Talk to a teacher or doctor. Avoid drugs/alcohol.

The Mission

  1. Create a colourful map showing your life to date (Physical paper, slides, etc).
  2. Start with birth. Mark the ups and downs along your road.
  3. Identify the TRANSITIONS (How did you get through it? What did you learn?).
  4. Include 10 major events.
  5. Add: Dates, Places, Symbols, Pictures.

Take a photo of your drawing and upload it here!
